chunk_summary: This section discusses a one-page feeding diary with risk scoring for pediatric feeding disorders, referencing guidelines on faltering growth, complementary feeding, and pediatric feeding disorder frameworks.
learning_objectives: Understand the purpose and structure of a one-page feeding diary for pediatric feeding disorders.
Learn how to apply risk scoring to identify feeding-related concerns in children.
Familiarize with international guidelines on faltering growth and complementary feeding.
Integrate evidence-based practices for managing pediatric feeding disorders.
key_facts: The NICE NG75 guideline provides recommendations for recognizing and managing faltering growth in children.
WHO guidelines emphasize complementary feeding for infants and young children aged 6–23 months.
The IMNCI chart booklet from NHM India offers practical tools for child health management.
Pediatric feeding disorder is defined using a consensus framework (PubMed ID: 30366855).
Risk scoring in feeding diaries can help identify children at risk of malnutrition or feeding difficulties.
reference_quote: "Faltering growth: recognition and management (NICE NG75)" - https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng75

subtopic: ONE-PAGE FEEDING DIARY WITH RISK SCORING
